issues.objection: Tags on 7.22 (operator-system-coupling, essence-vs-implementation, deliberate-action) cohere with same-tag verses across chapters. Coheres with 7.21 (faith-yoked practice presupposed); coheres with 2.47's right-to-action / not-cause-of-fruit doctrine — the engineer does the action, the upstream substrate produces the result. STRETCHED tagging keeps the operational scope honest where metaphysical reach exceeds it. The STRETCHED tag is honest for this verse: Verse claims Krishna is the actual granter (mayaiva vihitān); engineering analog operates at proximate-vs-upstream attribution scope (the framework is the vehicle; the customer-need-and-substrate is what actually grants the result). Counter-example carves out accidental success without disciplined practice.

notes: Doctrine-coherence test for 7.22: same-tag verses across chapters preserve consistent stance; the verse extends the doctrine without contradicting prior chapters. PASS.
